items.briefing:
Australian cyber experts are warning of a growing surge in third-party supplier risks, meaning organisations can be compromised through their vendors even if their own systems are secure. Separately, Australians are being reminded to stay alert to scams during major life events such as moving house, having a baby, or changing jobs — moments when scammers exploit distraction and urgency. A new study also found many Australian office workers are ignoring their organisation's AI usage policies, which can expose sensitive data.

A newly discovered ransomware strain called JadePuffer uses AI to automate attacks, making threats faster and harder to detect. **Practical tip:** Review your organisation's policies on AI tools and remind staff that using unapproved apps — even helpful-seeming ones — can put sensitive data at risk.

featured.title: Australian cyber experts warn of third-party risk surge
featured.quote: Third-party vendor breaches expose critical gaps in Australian school and business security.
